response:
My favorite book is like holding a smooth, sea-worn stone in your palm—cool, quiet, yet humming with depths you can’t see. It doesn’t shout; it whispers. You follow a character whose life feels utterly ordinary at first: morning routines, small disappointments, the weight of unsaid things between people. But as you read, the ordinary becomes luminous. The author finds profound meaning in the way light falls on a kitchen floor at 7 a.m., in the silence after a phone call ends, in how grief reshapes the space where love used to live.  

It taught me to notice the sacred in the overlooked—the tide pulling back to reveal hidden shells, the courage in simply showing up when your heart feels like broken pottery. There’s no grand adventure, no villain to defeat
